sections.name: Subjective
sections.text: The patient, a thirty-nine-year-old individual, has been diagnosed with stage four breast cancer, which has metastasized to the lungs and bones. She has been undergoing palliative chemotherapy for the past four years and is currently on a fourth-line chemotherapy agent. Initially, she experienced arm pain, which was managed effectively, and she reports no current issues with arm pain. She has been admitted to the hospital for infections and chemotherapy treatments. Despite her condition, she enjoys outdoor activities and has a dog. Her partner supports her end-of-life care preferences.

sections.name: Objective
sections.text:
Diagnosis: Stage four breast cancer with metastasis to lungs and bones
Chemotherapy: Fourth line agent ongoing
Pain Management: No current arm pain
Hospital Admission: History of admission for infections and chemotherapy
Treatment Preferences: Intravenous treatments for pneumonia, open to blood transfusions
CPR Preferences: Desired if reversible cause and good baseline function, not desired in natural dying process

sections.name: Assessment
sections.text: Stage four breast cancer with metastasis to lungs and bones. Ongoing chemotherapy and effective pain management. Palliative care preferences include selective CPR and willingness for intravenous treatments.

sections.name: Plan
sections.text:
Continue fourth line chemotherapy.
Administer intravenous treatments for pneumonia as needed.
Open to blood transfusions if required.
CPR desired if reversible cause and good baseline function. No CPR desired in natural dying process.
